what_it_does: A hand-authored shared browser runtime for every DNA surface: color hex math, HSL lightness inversion, warmth/polarity surface derivation, CSS variable injection, a local /api client, HTML escape, fallback token block, ordinal ramp coloring, an Atlas navigation chip, story tree traversal, control record rendering, a unified feedback mark model, spacing resolution/injection, role resolution, split-ratio/pane utilities, and pinch-zoom — all merged into global.DNA.
observations: Self-describes as the single shared runtime that surfaces should load instead of reimplementing lerp or hardcoding variables.
Uses Object.assign(global.DNA || {}, ...) to merge exports, with an explicit comment that a previous hard assign wiped prior definitions.
Implements dark polarity by HSL lightness inversion (L → 1−L) while keeping hue/saturation, deriving dark grounds from light ones.
Provides a local API client for /api/feedback, /api/tokens, /api/grammar, /api/layouts, /api/sequence, /api/dna/* on :8090.
Contains a generated fallback token block between DNA:FALLBACKS markers for surfaces that do not load surface.css.
Mounts an Atlas UI chip from surface/atlas.json that links surfaces, live faces, documents, and proofs.
Includes story tree traversal (node lookup and tier cuts) and a control renderer that builds buttons from application.components records.
Defines a unified mark model and marksOf() lens over legacy feedback storage shapes.
Space resolution derives px from grammar bonds, a density-dial multiplier, and a tier scale; injectSpace writes CSS variables live.
resolve() walks dotted pointers across tokens/grammar/roles/application and honestly flags space as unresolved pending per-relationship tuning.
splitPane is a general resizable/fullscreenable two-slot component with optional localStorage persistence and orientation-aware dragging.
pinchZoom is a generic two-finger zoom mechanism reusable by any surface.
standouts: Namespace merge instead of overwrite, fixing a load-order bug.
Dark polarity derived by HSL lightness inversion rather than a separate dark palette.
General splitPane and pinchZoom utilities intended for reuse by any surface.
Atlas chip mounted on every surface for universal estate navigation.
Role resolver with an explicit unresolved list rather than faking values.
conventions: Uses an IIFE in strict mode and attaches exports to window/global.DNA.
Merges into the existing DNA namespace with Object.assign.
Uses var(--x, fallback) CSS patterns with hardcoded fallbacks from the FB block.
Async fetch wrappers use cache: no-store for GETs and JSON bodies for POSTs.
Pure utility functions receive registries as arguments rather than fetching internally.
Comments reference external documents/packets (Packet 01 Block I, RC-B, RC-C, mob-21, etc.).
Mixes a generated fallback token block with hand-authored runtime code.
concerns: The hardcoded fallback token block may drift from canonical tokens despite the warning not to edit it.
API paths are relative and assume a :8090 server with no retry or offline handling.
resolve() pointer regex only allows letters, digits, and underscores, possibly rejecting valid keys with hyphens.
splitPane localStorage persistence may fail silently in sandboxed or private contexts.
mountAtlas injects CSS and DOM elements unconditionally, which may conflict with surfaces lacking expected fonts or body structure.
control() applies geometry and color as inline styles, making CSS overrides harder.
Limited input validation on nested token/grammar structures beyond null checks.
notes: This is the shared browser runtime every DNA surface loads for live-derived color, spacing, layout mechanics, story traversal, and estate navigation.
questions: Where are the canonical tokens.json, grammar.json, roles.json, and application.json files located and how are they loaded?
Which surfaces load this file and in what order relative to other DNA scripts?
How is the :8090 feedback/API server started and what backend does it persist to?
What are the exact schemas for application.components records and grammar.scales.space.bonds?
How does dark polarity interact with the generated surface.css variables?
What is the intended behavior when multiple surfaces or iframes each call mountAtlas on the same document?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches; it is the shared runtime it claims to be, and the additional UI mechanisms (Atlas, splitPane, pinchZoom) still fit the shared-runtime role.
apparent_themes: Derive values from registries rather than hardcoding them.
One shared runtime over per-surface reimplementation.
Honest about unresolved/tuning gaps instead of inventing values.
Reusable mechanisms usable by any surface.
Live reconfiguration through CSS custom properties.
intention_signals: Centralize all cross-surface design logic and reusable interaction mechanics in one file so surfaces stay consistent and can be live-tuned from DNA registries.
novelty: Combines a design-system runtime with HSL lightness-inversion dark mode, live spacing bond resolution, role pointer resolution, and general touch-aware split/pinch utilities in a single hand-authored shared file.
contribution: The shared color/spacing/role resolution engine, warmth/polarity surface derivation, CSS variable injection paths, reusable splitPane and pinchZoom mechanisms, the Atlas navigation chip, and the namespace-merge pattern.
summary_for_embedding: surface.js is a hand-authored shared browser runtime for the DNA design system that exposes color interpolation, warmth/polarity surface derivation, CSS variable injection, registry API access, story traversal, spacing resolution, role resolution, and reusable split-pane/pinch-zoom/atlas UI mechanisms via a merged global.DNA namespace.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to give every DNA surface a single source of live-derived design logic and reusable interaction mechanisms so nothing reimplements color, spacing, or layout mechanics.

symbols.does: Parses a hex color string into an [r,g,b] array.
symbols.description: Strips the leading hash, expands three-character shorthand, and returns decimal red/green/blue components used by the color math utilities.

symbols.does: Converts decimal RGB values to an uppercase hex string.
symbols.description: Clamps each channel to [0,255], rounds, zero-pads, and prepends a hash; the inverse of hexToRgb.

symbols.does: Helper inside rgbToHex that formats one color channel.
symbols.description: Clamps, rounds, and hex-pads a single numeric channel so rgbToHex can concatenate the result.

symbols.does: Linearly interpolates between two hex colors.
symbols.description: Converts both colors to RGB, mixes each channel by parameter t, and returns the resulting hex via rgbToHex.

symbols.does: Validates whether a string looks like a six-digit hex color.
symbols.description: Accepts an optional leading hash and is used by injectVars to decide whether a token should be lightness-inverted.

symbols.does: Normalizes a hex string to uppercase with a leading hash.
symbols.description: Trims whitespace and ensures consistent formatting for stored color values.

symbols.does: Converts a hex color to HSL components.
symbols.description: Computes hue, saturation, and lightness from RGB, supporting the invertL operation that derives dark-mode grounds.

symbols.does: Converts HSL components back to a hex color.
symbols.description: Implements the standard HSL-to-RGB algorithm and returns an uppercase hex string.

symbols.does: Piecewise helper inside hslToHex that maps a hue offset to RGB.
symbols.description: Performs the sector-based HSL-to-RGB interpolation for a single channel.

symbols.does: Inverts only the lightness channel of a hex color in HSL space.
symbols.description: Keeps hue and saturation unchanged and maps L to 1−L, preserving the magnitude of lightness differences between surfaces.

symbols.does: Generates the four page surface colors from warmth and polarity dials.
symbols.description: Lerps between cool and warm anchors for page/warm/cool/nested keys, then inverts lightness if polarity is dark.

symbols.does: Writes derived identity CSS variables onto a document or element root.
symbols.description: Computes surfaces, applies luminance-role inversion for dark polarity, and sets color/semantic/scaffold variables so a mockup iframe can be live-driven.

symbols.does: Local helper that inverts hex colors only when dark polarity is active.
symbols.description: Leaves chroma-role accents untouched and applies invertL only to valid hex strings.

symbols.does: Generic async GET helper for the local :8090 API.
symbols.description: Fetches JSON with no-store caching and throws on non-OK responses.

symbols.does: Generic async POST helper for the local :8090 API.
symbols.description: Sends JSON, parses the response, and throws if the server reports failure.

symbols.does: HTML-escapes a small set of characters for safe insertion.
symbols.description: Replaces &, <, >, and " with their named entities; used by Atlas and other rendering paths.

symbols.does: Maps an index in a collection to a color along the warm-metal ramp.
symbols.description: Piecewise lerps across ramp stops so positions in a list receive distinct hues.

symbols.does: Injects an Atlas navigation chip and slide-out panel into the current document.
symbols.description: Loads surface/atlas.json, fetches live face versions, and renders links to surfaces, faces, documents, and proofs.

symbols.does: Async builder for the Atlas panel contents.
symbols.description: Fetches atlas and per-face versions, then constructs the grouped HTML for surfaces, faces, docs, and proofs.

symbols.does: Shows the Atlas scrim and panel, building it on first use.
symbols.description: Toggles the CSS show classes that reveal the navigation overlay.

symbols.does: Hides the Atlas scrim and panel.
symbols.description: Removes the show classes and can be triggered by Escape key or scrim click.

symbols.does: Renders a button from an application.components control record.
symbols.description: Looks up the named component, selects a state, and applies geometry and color tokens as inline styles.

symbols.does: Factory for a typed feedback mark.
symbols.description: Returns a {target, kind, value} object representing a preference, verdict, note, or pin.

symbols.does: Lenses a legacy feedback entry into an array of unified marks.
symbols.description: Converts preferred/verdict/note/comments fields into additive mark records without changing storage shape.

symbols.does: Returns dial names declared to modify a given target.
symbols.description: Reads grammar.dials[*].targets.modifies to discover which dials affect spacing or other roles.

symbols.does: Computes the combined spacing multiplier from all relevant dials.
symbols.description: Multiplies per-dial multipliers for every dial whose modifies target is spacing.

symbols.does: Resolves a spatial bond into a pixel gap.
symbols.description: Multiplies the grammar unit by the bond count, density dial multiplier, and tier scale; returns zero if grammar is missing.

symbols.does: Writes live spacing CSS variables onto a document root.
symbols.description: Resolves count-named and bond-named space addresses through the density/tier dials so consumers re-space when dials change.

symbols.does: Resolves a role record into concrete color and type values from registries.
symbols.description: Walks dotted pointers against tokens/grammar/roles/application and returns resolved values plus an unresolved list; currently flags space as pending tuning.

symbols.does: Local pointer-walker used by resolve.
symbols.description: Splits a dotted path and traverses the provided registry objects, returning undefined for malformed or missing paths.

symbols.does: Derives a [work,panel] grow ratio from a viewer_layout configuration.
symbols.description: Maps a named proportion archetype and major side to low/high parts, with endpoints for full-work or full-panel modes.

symbols.does: Creates a general resizable/fullscreenable two-slot split.
symbols.description: Manages fraction state, optional localStorage persistence, orientation-aware divider dragging, and full-slot toggling.

symbols.does: Persists the current split fraction to localStorage.
symbols.description: Called after drag end and reset; silently ignores storage errors.

symbols.does: Clamps a fraction to the configured splitPane bounds.
symbols.description: Defaults to 0.5 when null and enforces the min/max clamp array.

symbols.does: Applies the current split state to the primary/secondary elements.
symbols.description: Sets flex percentages or hides one slot when fullscreen, and invokes the optional onApply callback.

symbols.does: Pointer-move handler for splitPane divider dragging.
symbols.description: Computes the fraction from pointer position relative to the split container and reapplies layout.

symbols.does: Pointer-up handler that ends splitPane dragging.
symbols.description: Removes global listeners and persists the final fraction.

symbols.does: Attaches a two-finger pinch-zoom gesture to an element.
symbols.description: Tracks touch distance ratio, applies a clamped zoom value via caller-supplied get/set, and notifies start/end callbacks.

symbols.does: Computes the Euclidean distance between two touch points.
symbols.description: Used by pinchZoom to measure the initial and current pinch span.

symbols.does: Ends an active pinch-zoom gesture when fingers lift.
symbols.description: Resets the pinch flag and invokes the optional onEnd callback.
